overview: This mono-white aggro deck leverages low-cost creatures and immediate disruption to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize in the Pauper format. By combining graveyard recursion with attrition-based growth, it maintains relentless pressure throughout the game. The strategy relies on efficient resource usage and denying opponent answers to secure quick victories.
win_conditions: Direct combat damage from flying and high-toughness creatures
Snowballing +1/+1 counters on Hinterland Sanctifier through creature attrition
Graveyard recursion via Kor Skyfisher to replay key threats and maintain board presence

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two one- or two-mana creatures
Early Novice Inspector to disrupt opponent's game plan
Kor Skyfisher paired with a cheap creature in hand or graveyard
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early board presence or interaction
Mana-heavy hands with only three-drops or higher
Hands without a clear path to apply pressure by turn three

early_game: Deploy one- and two-mana creatures to establish immediate board presence
Cast Novice Inspector to remove key answers or threats from opponent's hand
Prioritize playing creatures over holding lands to accelerate damage output
mid_game: Use Kor Skyfisher to return dead creatures from the graveyard to the battlefield
Trigger Hinterland Sanctifier's ability by sacrificing or losing creatures to grow a win condition
Maintain board pressure while denying opponent stabilization tools
late_game: Focus on growing Hinterland Sanctifier into an unblockable threat
Recycle high-impact creatures repeatedly with Kor Skyfisher
Close out the game with aggressive combat tricks and flying threats

key_cards.name: Kor Skyfisher
key_cards.notes: Use it to replay one-mana creatures like token producers or early attackers, enabling immediate triggers and maintaining pressure.

key_cards.name: Hinterland Sanctifier
key_cards.role: Attrition-based win condition and board presence
key_cards.notes: Let it grow by trading creatures or using sacrifice outlets; prioritize keeping it alive to become a large, difficult-to-remove threat.

key_cards.name: Novice Inspector
key_cards.notes: Cast it early to remove key answers, combo pieces, or removal spells, ensuring your aggressive board state remains unchallenged.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize casting Novice Inspector before playing creatures to remove answers that could interact with your board
Hold up mana for Kor Skyfisher's triggered ability if you have cheap creatures in the graveyard ready to replay
Sequence creature deaths to maximize Hinterland Sanctifier's growth before committing to a full board trade
Play flying threats last in combat to avoid being blocked by ground creatures

matchups.advice: Apply maximum early pressure and use Novice Inspector to remove key sweepers or counterspells. Recycle creatures with Skyfisher to outvalue their resource advantage.
Race them by growing Hinterland Sanctifier quickly and denying their removal with early disruption. Avoid trading your best threats unnecessarily.
Use Novice Inspector to remove their key threats and play flying creatures to bypass their board. Recycle blockers with Skyfisher to stabilize.
Disrupt their combo pieces immediately with Novice Inspector and apply lethal pressure before they can assemble their win condition.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Mono-White Aggro archetype and Pauper format constraints

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards Kor Skyfisher and Hinterland Sanctifier

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Mechanics of Novice Inspector, Kor Skyfisher, and Hinterland Sanctifier

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard Pauper format interactions and mono-white aggro vulnerabilities
